SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.rupture

Class PipeFireRuptureScenario

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.rupture.PipeFireRuptureScenario
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class PipeFireRuptureScenario extends Object implements Serializable
Fire exposure definition for blowdown pipe fire-rupture heat-up calculations.

The scenario mirrors the radiative plus convective heat-flux expression used in the strain-rate workbook and exposes all heat-transfer inputs explicitly for evidence review.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • PipeFireRuptureScenario

      public PipeFireRuptureScenario(String name, double fireTemperatureC, double gasTemperatureC, double convectiveHeatTransferCoefficientWPerM2K, double fireEmissivity, double metalEmissivity, double metalAbsorptivity, double passiveProtectionHeatFluxFactor)
      Creates a pipe fire exposure scenario.
      Parameters:
      name - scenario name; defaults to pipe fire when null or empty
      fireTemperatureC - radiating fire temperature in degrees Celsius
      gasTemperatureC - convecting gas temperature in degrees Celsius
      convectiveHeatTransferCoefficientWPerM2K - convective heat transfer coefficient in W/m2-K; must be non-negative
      fireEmissivity - fire emissivity from 0 to 1
      metalEmissivity - metal emissivity from 0 to 1
      metalAbsorptivity - metal absorptivity from 0 to 1
      passiveProtectionHeatFluxFactor - heat-flux multiplier from 0 to 1 after PFP/insulation
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any input is outside its valid range

  • Method Details

    • spreadsheetLargeJetFire

      public static PipeFireRuptureScenario spreadsheetLargeJetFire()
      Creates the workbook's large-jet-fire scenario.
      Returns:
      large jet fire scenario

      Creates the workbook's pool-fire scenario.
      Returns:
      pool fire scenario

    • withPassiveProtectionFactor

      public PipeFireRuptureScenario withPassiveProtectionFactor(double heatFluxFactor)
      Returns a copy with passive fire protection or insulation applied.
      Parameters:
      heatFluxFactor - heat-flux multiplier from 0 to 1
      Returns:
      copied scenario with the new heat-flux factor
